segments.text: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, consider
the Apostle and High Priest of our profession, Christ Jesus
segments.speaker: Narrator

segments.text: Who was faithful to him that appointed him
as also Moses was faithful in all his house
For this man was counted worthy of more glory than Moses, inasmuch as he who hath builded the house hath more honour than the house
For every house is builded by some man
but he that built all things is God
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And Moses verily was faithful in all his house, as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken after
But Christ as a son over his own house
whose house are we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end
segments.speaker: Christ

segments.text: To day if ye will hear his voice
segments.speaker: Holy Ghost

segments.text: Harden not your hearts, as in the provocation, in the day of temptation in the wilderness
segments.speaker: Holy Ghost

segments.text: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works forty years
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Wherefore I was grieved with that generation, and said, They do alway err in their heart; and they have not known my ways
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So I sware in my wrath, They shall not enter into my rest
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, in departing from the living God
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But exhort one another daily, while it is called To day; lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence stedfast unto the end
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: While it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation
For some, when they had heard, did provoke: howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses
But with whom was he grieved forty years? was it not with them that had sinned, whose carcases fell in the wilderness?
And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest, but to them that believed not?
So we see that they could not enter in because of unbelief
